english: take, to hire for a servant Pth.; frq. fig. séms-kyi kól-por yda (sems kyi khol por yda) (the body) is a servant of the mind Mil.; ǰig-rtén sriú-pai kól-po (jig rten srid pai khol po) a servant of the world i.e. of mammon Mil.
a small piece, kól-bu nyúṅ-bru tsam źig kyaṅ ma lus (khol bu nyung bru tsam zhig kyang ma lus) Pth. not so much as a grain of mustard seed is left.

1. Cs. 'anything boiled'; perh. more accurately: anything boiling, ču kól-ma (chu khol ma) boiling water; dúg-mtso kól-ma (dug mtsho khol ma) a boiling lake of poison. - 2. Sch.: an outlet for the smoke in a roof.
1. maid-servant B. - 2. a coarse sort of blanket usually given to slaves Schr. - 3. mowed corn, a swath C.
Cs., *Ká-le* (*Kha le*) W., as much as fills the hollow of the hand, a handful, e.g. of water.

1. frozen; ice. - 2. the frost, cold, kyág-ťog-kar (khyag thog kar) on the ice Glr.; kyág-pa kyág-pai bód-yul (khyag pa khyag pai bod yul) 'Tibet frozen up with frost' Pth.; *kyág-la ǰar* (*khyag la jar*) (v. byór-ba (byor ba)) *soṅ* (*song*) W. it has stuck fast by freezing. - *kyag-žu-ko-kó* (*khyag zhu ko ko*) Ts. mud caused by a thaw, snow-water. - *kyág-sran-čan* (*khyag sran can*) W. hardened against the cold. - kyag-rúm (khyag rum), kyag-róm (khyag rom) ice, pieces of ice, floating blocks of ice (also čab-róm (chab rom)); cf. kyag-pa (khyag pa).

1. difference, distinction B., C., W. *gaṅ taṅ-na kyad med* (*gang tang na khyad med*) W. it is no matter which you give me; ṅá-daṅ-prad-pa daṅ kyad-méd-do (nga dang phrad pa dang khyad med do) it is quite the same as if they came to myself; séms-la kyad byuṅ (sems la khyad byung) a difference of opinion arose. - kyad-čos (khyad chos) mark of distinction. - 2. something excellent, superior, bzoi kyad (bzoi khyad), bzo-kyád (bzo khyad) an excellent work of art Glr.; bsgrúb-pai kyad yoṅ (bsgrub pai khyad yong) prob.: it shall be instantly performed in the very best manner Pth. - kyad-nór (khyad nor) the principal or chief wealth Cs. - kyad-dóṅ (khyad don) the principal sense Sch. - 3. syllable employed to form abstract nouns. A transition to such formations appears in the following sentence: dkar-nag-čós-kyi če-kyád blta (dkar nag chos kyi che khyad blta) Mil. we wish to examine the difference of greatness or worth of the white and the black religion; so also whenever a certain measure is given, and in general, when such abstract nouns are used in a relative sense, as: mťo-kyád (mtho khyad) height, zab-kyád (zab khyad) depth, pyug-kyád (phyug khyad) wealth. - 4. part, division, the same as kyád-par (khyad par) .2; *sa-kyád* (*sa khyad*) W. place, corresponding exactly to *sa-ču* (*sa chu*) C. Derivatives. *kyé'-tsar-čèn* (*khye tsar chen*) = ṅó-mtsar-čan (ngo mtshar can) wonderful C. - kyád-du (khyad du) adv. especially, particularly, kyád-du págs-pa (khyad du phags pa) particularly (uncommonly) lofty, sublime Glr. kyád-par (khyad par) adv. = kyád-du (khyad du) Glr. 50, 7, and more frq. sbst.: 1. difference, dissimilarity B. and C., ṅa daṅ kyod ynyis kyád-par-če (nga dang khyod gnyis khyad par che) I and you - that is a great difference Glr.; de daṅ kyád-par-ma-mčís-pai rten (de dang khyad par ma mchis pai rten) an image not differing from this Glr.; miṅ-gi kyád-par (ming gi khyad par) yin it is (only) a difference of name Glr. - 2. sort, kind, brás-bui kyád-par kun (bras bui khyad par kun) all sorts of fruit; ri-dvágs-kyi kyád-par źig (ri dwags kyi khyad par zhig) a particular kind of game; perh. also: division, part, yúl-gyi kyád-par (yul gyi khyad par) province Tar. 20. 14. - 3. = kyad (khyad) 2. something of superior qualities, an excellent man Tur. 30, 7. kyád-par-čan (khyad par can) superior, excellent, capital, blá-ma kyád-par-čan čig (bla ma khyad par can cig) Mil. an excellent spiritual teacher; kyád-par-du (khyad par du) adv. particularly, chiefly, especially. Rather obscure as to its literal sense, but of frq. use is the phrase kyád-du ysód-pa (khyad du gsod pa), ysád-pa (gsad pa), c. accus. but also dat., to despise, e.g. dmá-la (dma' la) an inferior, rgyu-brás (rgyu bras) the doctrine of retribution, nyon-móṅs-pa (nyon mongs pa) trouble etc.

to fill, penetrate; to embrace, comprise, c. accus., also dat., mi-gtsáṅ-bas kyáb-pai sa-pyógs (mi gtsang bas khyab pai sa phyogs) a place full of dirt Thgy.; brúm-pa máṅ-pos (brum pa mang pos) full of, quite covered with pustules, pocks Med.; mkris- (mkhris-)
